input.background: Analyze the distribution of the residuals in the grandfather clock example, both before and after the outlier residual is removed. Determine whether the assumption of a normally distributed error term is reasonable.
input.question: Analyze the histogram and normal probability plot to determine the impact of the outlier on the distribution of residuals.

output.answer: Note that the outlier appears to skew the histogram in Figure 12.45, whereas the histogram in Figure 12.46 appears to be more mound-shaped. Similarly, the pattern of residuals in the normal probability plot in Figure 12.46 (outlier deleted) is more nearly a straight line than the pattern in Figure 12.45 (outlier included).
